Schedule of Students In Charge

Since the 2004–2005 academic year, the "Student Assistant Programme" has been in operation at our University. The "Student Assistant Programme" is based on the principle of enabling students to gain work experience by working within the University environment during their studies, to develop socially and culturally, to become better acquainted with the University’s various departments, and to earn a modest income. Applications are announced annually at the start of the academic year via email, social media and our noticeboards. Under this programme, our students may work a maximum of 60 hours per month and receive a nominal hourly rate in return.

Student Employee Insurance is provided in accordance with Article 5(b) of the Social Insurance and General Health Insurance Law No. 5510; For students employed on a part-time basis under Article 46 of the Higher Education Act No. 2547, where the monthly income subject to contributions does not exceed thirty times the daily income threshold determined under Article 82, work accident and occupational disease insurance applies. Those listed in this subparagraph are deemed insured under subparagraph (a) of the first paragraph of Article 4.

Units Where the Student Assistant Programme is Implemented:

•	Directorate of Health, Culture and Sports Services

•	Directorate of Information Processing

•	Public Relations and Communications Unit

•	Library and Documentation Centre

•	International Relations Office

•	Control and Automation Laboratory

•	Electronic Communications Laboratory

•	Student Affairs Directorate

•	Institute of Postgraduate Education

•	Centre for Research, Innovation, Application and Advanced Technology
